context.product: Consumer meal-planning app that creates a weekly plan and shopping list; natural use cycle is weekly
context.customer: Primary users and payers are English-speaking adults who plan household meals; the priority segment is new free and trial users acquired through direct, organic, referral, and paid channels
context.business_model: Freemium consumer subscription with monthly and annual premium plans; value is measured by completed weekly meal plans and shopping lists

outcome.metric: Week-4 retained weekly value: a new user saves a meal plan and completes at least five shopping-list items in both week 1 and week 4
outcome.segment: Eligible new users after employee, test, bot, and fraudulent-install exclusions; acquisition source includes an explicit unknown bucket
outcome.time_window: Plan to enroll from 2026-07-27 through 2026-08-10; if fewer than 6,000 eligible users are randomized by August 10, continue until 6,000 and move the final decision to at least 32 days after enrollment completes. Otherwise decide no earlier than 2026-09-11 after the last cohort has 28 days of observation and three days of processing
outcome.symptom: Monthly new users grew from 120,000 in January-February 2026 to 310,000 in May-June after acquisition scaled, while mature week-4 retained weekly value fell from 31.6% to 21.4%; the rounded planning baseline is 21% and the target is 27%

metrics.name: Current acquisition weight: organic and referral
Current week-4 value: organic and referral
metrics.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohorts

metrics.name: Current week-4 value: core paid
metrics.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohorts

metrics.name: Current week-4 value: influencer short-video
metrics.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohorts

metrics.name: Current week-4 value: unknown source
metrics.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohorts

constraint.primary: The app no longer delivers a sufficiently relevant and reliable weekly plan for many new users, so the recurring meal-planning value disappears before week 4
constraint.mechanism: Rapid catalog and ranking changes increased unavailable, duplicate, and irrelevant recipe results while search and list-sync failures rose. Users complete fewer useful plans in week 1, encounter less novelty and more repair work in later weeks, and stop returning on the natural weekly cycle. Higher notification exposure then reminds users of weak value and accelerates opt-out rather than restoring it.
constraint.selection_rationale: Using the same all-eligible-user denominator as the headline, prior weights of 45% organic/referral, 40% core paid, 6% influencer short-video, and 9% unknown applied to prior rates of 35%, 31%, 20%, and 25% produce 31.6%. Current weights of 30%, 25%, 36%, and 9% applied to current rates of 29%, 25%, 13%, and 20% produce 21.4%, a 10.2-point decline. Applying prior weights to current rates produces 25.6%, so mix explains 4.2 points and within-source or product effects explain the remaining 6.0 points. Within each source, week-1 plan-and-list completion predicts week-4 value, and completion, content quality, search reliability, and sync reliability deteriorated. Lost recurring value therefore has more explanatory reach than acquisition quality alone, while observational evidence keeps confidence below high certainty.

alternative_constraints.constraint: Lower acquisition quality after influencer short-video scaling
alternative_constraints.reason_not_primary: Influencer short-video users retain worse and the auditable mix shift explains 4.2 points of the decline, but current source rates under the prior mix still imply only 25.6% retention versus 31.6% previously. Acquisition quality is material but does not explain the larger within-source or product loss.

alternative_constraints.constraint: Notification fatigue causes users to disengage
alternative_constraints.reason_not_primary: Push opt-out rose and users exposed to five or more pushes retain worse, but notification frequency is partly triggered by inactivity and poor plan completion. The prespecified restoration-versus-notification comparison will test whether restoring value outperforms reducing reminders.

alternative_constraints.constraint: Product reliability failures are the primary cause
alternative_constraints.reason_not_primary: Crash, search, and list-sync failures are associated with low retention, but detected severe failures affect 28% of new users and cannot alone explain the full decline. Reliability is treated as part of restoring recurring value and will be reported separately.

The decline persists after comparing the same sources and household-intent segments, and weekly grocery-list creation fell before the seasonal holiday period. No matched prior-year cohort is available, so seasonality remains unresolved.

supporting.finding: Prior weights of 45% organic/referral, 40% core paid, 6% influencer short-video, and 9% unknown applied to prior rates of 35%, 31%, 20%, and 25% produce 31.6%. Current weights of 30%, 25%, 36%, and 9% applied to current rates of 29%, 25%, 13%, and 20% produce 21.4%, a 10.2-point decline. Prior weights applied to current rates produce 25.6%; mix explains 4.2 points and within-source or product effects explain the remaining 6.0 points.
supporting.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohort model
supporting.segment: All mature eligible new-user cohorts, including unknown source
supporting.quality_notes: The unknown bucket is retained in the common denominator. Displayed inputs reconcile after rounding to one decimal. The decomposition is descriptive, not incremental.

supporting.finding: May-June volume averaged 310,000 new users per month, and 92% passed the planned pre-randomization employee, test, bot, fraud, and eligibility filters, or about 9,500 eligible users per day. Randomizing 6,000 users during the planned 15-day window therefore requires about 1.1 days of observed eligible volume at a conservative 60% experiment-allocation cap.
supporting.quality_notes: Future volume and eligibility mix may change; enrollment continues and the final date moves if 6,000 assignments are not reached by August 10.

supporting.finding: Week-4 retained weekly value fell from 35% to 29% for organic/referral, 31% to 25% for core paid, 20% to 13% for influencer short-video, and 25% to 20% for unknown source between January-February and May-June.
supporting.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohort model
supporting.quality_notes: Cohorts are mature and definitions are stable; creative, geography, and household-intent mix still vary within source.

supporting.finding: Users who saved a plan and completed at least five shopping-list items in week 1 reached 48% week-4 retained weekly value, versus 12% for users who saved a plan without completing the list. Week-1 weekly value completion fell from 64% to 49% across the comparison periods.
supporting.quality_notes: The association is observational and may reflect intent; event coverage is 97% and stable.

supporting.finding: Recipe search zero-result rate rose from 6% to 14%, unavailable-recipe impressions rose from 4% to 13%, and list-sync failure rose from 2% to 7%. Users with any detected severe quality failure reached 9% week-4 value versus 24% without one; 28% of May-June new users had a detected failure.
supporting.source: Search, catalog, sync, and weekly value events
supporting.quality_notes: Silent failures and subjective relevance are undercounted; affected users may attempt more complex plans.

supporting.finding: In 20 interviews with May-June users who completed a week-1 plan but did not retain to week 4, 14 described repetitive or irrelevant recommendations and 11 described unavailable recipes, duplicate ingredients, or list corrections that made a later plan not worth the effort.
supporting.segment: Week-1 value completers who did not retain to week 4
supporting.quality_notes: Purposive small sample; counts overlap and are not population estimates.

supporting.finding: Push opt-out by day 14 rose from 8% to 19%. Users receiving five or more pushes in their first 14 days reached 15% week-4 value versus 24% for users receiving one or two pushes.
supporting.source: Notification delivery, preference, and weekly value events
supporting.quality_notes: Exposure is confounded because inactivity triggers reminders; delivery does not prove attention.

supporting.finding: Estimated 90-day premium conversion is 8.2% for users with week-4 retained weekly value and 1.9% without it; the May-June retention decline is therefore expected to worsen paid acquisition payback if it persists.
supporting.source: Subscription model joined to weekly value cohorts
supporting.segment: Mature new users with billing eligibility
supporting.quality_notes: The 90-day estimate uses earlier mature cohorts and is not a causal LTV estimate.

contradictory.finding: Influencer short-video grew from 6% to 36% of all eligible acquisition and its May-June week-4 retained weekly value is 13%, directly supporting acquisition quality as a substantial competing explanation.
contradictory.source: Attribution joined to weekly value cohorts
contradictory.segment: All mature eligible new users, including unknown source
contradictory.quality_notes: Attribution is last-touch and lacks an incrementality holdout; the channel may also contain different household intent.

contradictory.finding: Among users who completed week-1 weekly value without any detected quality failure, week-4 retained value still fell from 52% to 45%, so measured product-quality failures do not explain all within-source deterioration.
contradictory.source: Quality telemetry joined to weekly value cohorts
contradictory.segment: Week-1 value completers without detected severe failures
contradictory.quality_notes: Relevance and novelty are incompletely measured, and telemetry cannot observe off-app changes in need.

contradictory.finding: Six of 20 interviewed churned users said they had no meal-planning need during travel, schedule changes, or eating out, independent of recommendation or reliability problems.
contradictory.segment: Week-1 value completers who did not retain to week 4
contradictory.quality_notes: Small purposive sample with recall bias; temporary lack of need may be seasonal.

missing_evidence: We lack randomized evidence separating restored recurring value from reduced notification exposure; it is needed before choosing product-quality work, lifecycle changes, or both for the default new-user experience.

We lack an acquisition incrementality holdout and stable creative-level intent labels for influencer short-video users; they are needed before reducing spend or setting a retained-value-adjusted channel budget.
We lack a matched prior-year weekly cohort and a reliable travel or schedule-change signal; they are needed to determine whether seasonality materially changes the primary diagnosis.
We lack complete server-side list-sync failure coverage and a validated recipe-relevance score; they are needed to estimate how much of lost recurring value is reliability versus content relevance.

priorities.action: Build and QA a weekly retention scorecard by acquisition source, creative intent, week-1 recurring-value completion, recipe relevance, availability, search failures, list-sync failures, and notification exposure.
priorities.rationale: Makes the acquisition-mix decomposition reproducible and closes the measurement gaps needed to interpret the experiment.
priorities.success_signal: At least 95% event coverage, source totals reconciled within 2 percentage points, and weekly mature-cohort reporting with quality and notification cuts.
dependencies: Attribution, catalog, search, sync, notification, and subscription joins
guardrails: Do not collect recipe free text, grocery-list contents, or health-condition data
Suppress reporting for cells with fewer than 100 mature users

priorities.action: Run a prespecified three-arm randomized test: current experience, recurring-value restoration, and notification cap only.
priorities.rationale: Tests whether restoration reaches the 27% target and powers the required direct comparison against the expected 23% notification-cap ITT rate.
priorities.success_signal: A final ITT decision is made only from mature week-4 outcomes after at least 6,000 eligible users are randomized, with approximately 2,000 assigned per arm; if enrollment finishes after August 10, the decision moves to at least 32 days after completion.
guardrails: Eligibility and employee, test, bot, and fraud exclusions occur before randomization; retain every assigned user in ITT and count missing week-4 outcomes as not retained
The 21%, 23%, and 27% planning rates already include expected missing outcomes counted as failures; do not apply an attrition discount
The August 14 review is data-quality and safety only, with no efficacy or futility decision
Pause an arm for crash safety only when the 95% confidence-interval upper bound for crash-free sessions is below 98.0% on two consecutive daily looks
Do not ship from proxy movement or increase sponsored-recipe impressions

priorities.action: Create a retained-value-adjusted acquisition review and pause only creatives whose mature week-4 value remains below 15% after intent and product exposure are controlled.
priorities.rationale: Addresses the material acquisition-quality contribution without mistaking it for the full retention mechanism.
priorities.success_signal: Every scaled creative has mature retained value, CAC per retained user, and an explicit keep, cap, or pause decision.
dependencies: Four weeks of mature creative cohorts
guardrails: Do not reallocate more than 20% of weekly spend before mature cohort review
Do not optimize to installs or day-1 engagement alone

priorities.action: After assigned users have mature week-4 outcomes, interview 12 participants across the three arms, acquisition source, and week-4 outcome; finish coding recurring-value, fatigue, and temporary-need-loss themes before the final decision.
priorities.rationale: Tests mechanisms that telemetry cannot distinguish and explains contradictory travel and seasonality evidence without contaminating treatment before outcome maturity.
priorities.success_signal: A coded evidence memo states which mechanism each arm changed and identifies any segment requiring a different treatment.
guardrails: Do not interview participants before their week-4 outcome matures
Do not request grocery-list contents, dietary diagnoses, or household member identities
Stop contact after an opt-out

experiments.hypothesis: Expected assigned-user ITT week-4 rates are 21% for control, 23% for notification cap only, and 27% for recurring-value restoration. Restoration will reach the target and outperform notification cap directly, showing that lost recurring value is more causal than notification fatigue.
experiments.intervention: Before randomization, apply eligibility rules and exclude employees, tests, bots, and fraudulent installs. Randomize at least 6,000 remaining users equally to three arms: control/current experience; recurring-value restoration that removes unavailable recipes before ranking, adds diversity constraints, retries list sync, and exposes recovery status; or notification cap only with at most two pushes in days 1-14. Analyze by intention to treat: every assigned user remains in the assigned arm, missing week-4 outcomes count as not retained, and no post-assignment exclusions are allowed. The 21%, 23%, and 27% planning rates are assigned-user ITT rates that already include expected missing outcomes as failures. Approximately 2,000 assigned users per arm gives about 80% power for the required 4-point restoration-versus-notification contrast at two-sided alpha 0.05 and greater power for restoration versus control. Use hierarchical prespecified testing: restoration-versus-control is the primary test; only if it passes, test restoration-versus-notification-cap as the direct mechanism comparison at two-sided alpha 0.05. Other source cuts are exploratory.
experiments.primary_metric: ITT week-4 retained weekly value: assigned users who complete the defined week-4 value event divided by all users assigned to that arm, with missing outcomes counted as not retained
experiments.decision_rule: The 2026-08-14 review is limited to assignment integrity, data quality, and safety; it makes no efficacy or futility decision. Do not report week-1 or week-2 proxies until at least 300 assigned users per arm have matured for that proxy, and treat any proxy report as descriptive only. Make the final decision on 2026-09-11 or later only after every enrolled cohort has a complete 28-day window. If fewer than 6,000 eligible users are randomized by August 10, continue enrollment and move the final decision to at least 32 days after enrollment completes. Ship restoration if its ITT rate is at least 27%, its absolute lift over control is at least 6 points with a two-sided 95% confidence interval excluding zero, and restoration also exceeds notification cap in the hierarchical prespecified direct comparison with a two-sided 95% confidence interval excluding zero. If restoration beats control but not notification cap, do not claim value restoration is primary and extend the experiment; do not change the default unless notification cap independently beats control with a two-sided 95% confidence interval excluding zero and all guardrails pass. If restoration does not beat control, stop and revisit acquisition quality, notification fatigue, seasonality, and unmeasured relevance before further product rollout.
guardrails: Pause an arm for crash safety only when the 95% confidence-interval upper bound for crash-free sessions is below 98.0% on two consecutive daily looks
Day-14 push opt-out no more than 3 percentage points above control
Week-1 weekly value no more than 2 percentage points below control
Support contacts per 1,000 users no more than 15% above control
No increase in sponsored-recipe impressions or collection of sensitive dietary data
No efficacy or futility stopping at the August 14 review
experiments.duration: Planned enrollment 2026-07-27 to 2026-08-10; data-quality and safety-only review 2026-08-14; interviews only after individual week-4 outcomes mature and coded by 2026-09-08; final decision no earlier than 2026-09-11, or at least 32 days after enrollment completes if 6,000 assignments are reached later

handbook_references.title: Retention Handbook
handbook_references.relevance: Defines recurring value, natural retention intervals, cohort analysis, and churn diagnosis.

handbook_references.title: Acquisition Handbook
handbook_references.relevance: Supports separating acquisition volume and channel quality from downstream retained demand.

handbook_references.title: Growth Metrics System
handbook_references.relevance: Connects leading behavior, cohort retention, CAC, and decision guardrails without rewarding low-quality acquisition.

case_references.title: Retention case comparison
case_references.relevance: Provides comparable retention mechanisms and evidence patterns; it does not supply metrics for this simulation.

case_references.title: Duolingo
case_references.relevance: Shows how recurring value, lifecycle messaging, and experimentation can interact in a consumer habit product; meal planning has a different natural cycle and all figures here are simulated.

case_references.title: Pinterest
case_references.relevance: Provides a reference for content relevance and notification decisions in a consumer discovery product; its context and metrics are not transferred to this simulation.

dependencies: First-party product, attribution, notification, reliability, and subscription data
Stable experiment assignment and cohort processing
Recipe ranking, catalog, sync, lifecycle, analytics, marketing, and research capacity
guardrails: Do not collect recipe free text, grocery-list contents, dietary diagnoses, or household identities
Apply all eligibility exclusions before randomization; use ITT with missing week-4 outcomes counted as not retained and no post-assignment exclusions
Pause an arm for crash safety only when the 95% confidence-interval upper bound for crash-free sessions is below 98.0% on two consecutive daily looks
Day-14 push opt-out may not exceed control by more than 3 percentage points
Week-1 weekly value may not fall more than 2 percentage points below control
Support contacts per 1,000 users may not rise more than 15%
The August 14 review has no efficacy or futility decision; do not make product decisions from immature week-4 cohorts
